seoTitle: Embed an Excel Document Within Confluence (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to embed an Excel document within a Confluence page in 2026 using the Office connector, so your team views a live, synced spreadsheet in place.
intro: Static file attachments go stale the moment someone updates the source spreadsheet elsewhere. This guide covers how to embed a live Excel document within a Confluence page in 2026 so viewers always see current data.

steps.heading: Open the upload block
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type `/excel` or `/file` to deploy the media and Office upload block.

steps.heading: Choose local or cloud
steps.body: Click Upload to drag a local .xlsx file in, or paste a Microsoft 365 or OneDrive sharing URL for a cloud-connected version.

steps.heading: Adjust the frame
steps.body: Click the embedded Excel frame to adjust the viewport height, toggle gridlines, and pick specific sheets.

steps.heading: Review and publish
steps.body: Review your layout and click Publish at the top right to save your settings and go live.

faq.question: What's the difference between embedding a local file versus a OneDrive link?
faq.answer: A local upload embeds a static snapshot, while a OneDrive or SharePoint link keeps the embedded view synced to live edits in the source file.

faq.question: Can I show only one tab from a multi-sheet workbook?
faq.answer: Yes, the viewer settings let you select a default sheet tab to display instead of the whole workbook.

faq.question: Will my formulas still work in the embedded view?
faq.answer: The embedded viewer renders the workbook's calculated values and layout, avoiding the formula loss that happens with basic copy-paste.

faq.question: Is there a single source of truth if multiple people edit the sheet?
faq.answer: Yes, the cloud sync connection is designed to give your team one live, up-to-date source of truth instead of duplicate copies.
